title: ptelea trifoliata var. lutescens
intro: Ptelea trifoliata var. lutescens, also known as the variegated or golden horsenettle, is a variety of the Ptelea trifoliata plant, which is a perennial shrub native to eastern North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving rheumatic pain, and treating skin irritations, among others. Ptelea trifoliata var. lutescens can be used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and salves, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
